name: Readymade Toilet Cabin
description:
A readymade toilet cabin is a sanitation unit that arrives at your site fully built and fitted, so the day it's unloaded is the day it can be used. There's no on-site fabrication, no panel assembly, and no waiting on a plumber to make it work. SAMAN builds and finishes the unit at our factory, delivers it whole, and all you connect is a water line.

What "Readymade" Actually Means for You

Readymade means the unit is built, plumbed internally, and finished before it leaves the factory. It arrives as one complete piece — pan, cistern, door, vent and internal piping already fitted. You don't construct anything on site. Place it on level ground, connect a water point, and it's a working toilet the same day.

Size, Material & What's Inside the Unit

A factory-gate sanitation point — one unit covers a high-traffic entry area.

The standard SAMAN unit is a single-seater cabin, 6 × 4 × 7 ft, built on a steel body for strength and a long service life on rough sites. Inside, you get a choice of Indian pan or Western commode, a flushing cistern, an internal water inlet, a roof vent for airflow, and a lockable door. The compact footprint suits a single high-use point — a site gate, a labour area, an event corner — where one clean, durable unit does the job. For projects that need several access points, units are supplied side by side rather than as one large block, keeping each one easy to clean and maintain.

What You Supply at Site & How Soon It's Usable

Connected and working — water line joined, drainage to a soak pit, ready the same day.

This is where a ready-to-use toilet cabin earns its name. Because the unit is pre-built, there are no plumbing delays — your only site preparation is a flat, firm surface and a nearby water connection point. Drainage runs to a soak pit, septic line, or holding tank depending on your site; tell us your setup and we'll confirm the right outlet before dispatch. Once the unit is positioned and the water line is joined, it's in service immediately. For most South India sites the cabin is delivered ready within days of order confirmation — a genuine no-install toilet cabin, not a kit you assemble.

Readymade Toilet Cabin Price — What's Included & What Moves It

A navy unit at a society-construction entrance — durable finish for longer site stays.

The single-seater is priced at ₹65,000, inclusive of taxes, covering the steel-built cabin, the fitted pan and cistern, internal piping, vent and door. What changes the readymade toilet cabin price is straightforward: Indian pan versus Western commode, whether you add an overhead water tank, and whether you need one unit or several positioned together. Transport is quoted separately by distance. There are no hidden fabrication or fitting charges — the unit you're quoted is the unit that arrives, finished.

Readymade vs Movable vs Prefabricated: Which Do You Need?

A high-visibility orange unit serving an outdoor event — easy to spot, quick to place.

All three are standalone sanitation units, but they answer different needs. Use this to pick the right one — and if you're still scoping the requirement, browse our full portable toilet range for the complete line.

What matters most to you Best fit
Working the same day, no on-site install Readymade toilet cabin (this unit)
Shifting the unit between sites as work moves a unit designed to relocate easily
Documented factory build and finish consistency our prefabricated toilet

If your priority is simply "deliver it ready, I don't want to install anything," the readymade unit on this page is the one.

Multiple units placed side by side for higher-traffic sites — each easy to clean separately.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a toilet cabin called?

It's commonly called a portable toilet, toilet cabin, or — when delivered pre-built and fitted like this one — a readymade toilet cabin. They all describe a standalone sanitation unit, separate from any office or living cabin.

Which type of toilet is best for a site unit?

For Indian construction and labour use, an Indian pan is the common, low-maintenance choice; for events or office-adjacent sites, a Western commode is often preferred. Both are available in this unit — pick by who uses it most.

How soon can I use it after delivery?

The same day. Once it's on level ground with a water connection joined, it's in service. There's no curing, assembly, or plumbing wait.

What's the difference between a readymade and a prefabricated toilet?

Readymade describes the buyer outcome — it arrives finished and works on arrival. Prefabricated describes how it's made — factory-built and quality-checked before dispatch. They overlap, but if build method and finish documentation matter most to you, the prefabricated unit is the better-matched page.

Get a quote in 24 hours. Tell us your site location, whether you need an Indian or Western unit, and how many — we'll send a clear, itemized price including transport. Call or WhatsApp 09708989937.
